abstract:
We introduce BEAST 2, an open-source, extensible software platform designed for Bayesian evolutionary analysis. This platform represents a comprehensive redesign of the widely used BEAST 1, specifically addressing structural limitations that emerged as the original software grew. A primary focus of this redesign was to overcome the lack of post-deployment extensibility. To achieve this, BEAST 2 features a robust package management system, enabling third-party developers to implement new functionalities that users can install directly without waiting for a core software release. 

We showcase this modular architecture through several recently published models, including birth-death-sampling tree priors, phylodynamic tools, and model-averaging techniques for substitution models and site partitioning. Another significant advancement is the platform’s ability to read and write the complete state of the MCMC chain to disk. This capability simplifies sharing between software instances and facilitates efficient checkpointing, providing better support for multi-processor and high-performance computing environments. Finally, we have achieved tighter integration between the analysis engine and the user interface (BEAUti 2). By utilizing a shared XML format and a template-based mechanism, new packages can now be seamlessly incorporated into the graphical interface, significantly enhancing user accessibility.
